title: EB-1A pack is the genuine ten incl. Artistic exhibitions AND Scholarly articles; no O-1A fallback on the live path
expected: Screened against the genuine EB-1A ten, with both 'Artistic exhibitions' AND 'Scholarly articles' present (the EB-1A-defining pairing), never the O-1A eight.
got: EB-1A pack defines exactly ten criteria including 'Scholarly articles' (packs.ts:152) and 'Artistic exhibitions' (packs.ts:153-158). packFor() falls back to O-1A only for unknown/unset classification (packs.ts:222-225); EB-1A is a live US program (jurisdictions.ts:63,101-103) so the fallback never fires. buildQualifyPrompt enumerates the ten by name (qualification.ts:120-141). Validation record states the ten match 8 CFR 204.5(h)(3)(i)-(x) verbatim in set and order (validation.ts:103-126).
suggested_acceptance: Keep the EB-1A pack exactly as-is (set + order); it is the spine of the EB-1A self-petitioner journey.
l2_priority: Confirm the live screening renders the ten with Artistic exhibitions and Scholarly articles both visible on-screen for an EB-1A run.

title: Best-path recognizes EB-1A as the green-card route in code
expected: The best-path / recommendation recognizes EB-1A as a green-card (permanent residence) route — the reason she chose it over O-1.
got: GREEN_CARD_PROGRAMS = new Set(['EB-1A']) (best-path.ts:29); rationaleFor appends 'It is also a green card (permanent residence).' (best-path.ts:120-121); the comparison card tags EB-1A '· green card' (BestPathFinder.tsx:241).
suggested_acceptance: Keep the green-card flag and rationale clause.
l2_priority: Confirm the live best-path recommendation banner shows the green-card line when EB-1A is recommended.

title: Keyword mock mis-buckets a biennale/gallery exhibition as Press due to pack-order first-match (mock path only)
expected: A biennale/gallery showing maps to the 'Artistic exhibitions' criterion, not to Press.
got: mockQualification tests criteria in pack order and returns the first regex match (qualification.ts:227-238). PRESS (criterion #3, /featured|.../, packs.ts:51) precedes 'Artistic exhibitions' (#7, /exhibit|exhibition|gallery|.../, packs.ts:155). A line like 'Featured at the Venice Architecture Biennale exhibition' matches Press before Artistic exhibitions. This is the keyless mock used by the landing certificate and best-path preview; under LLM_ENGINE=claude the real model classifies against the named ten.
suggested_acceptance: On the mock path, either reorder so the more specific arts criteria match before the broad Press/Original regexes, or make Artistic exhibitions' regex win on 'exhibition|biennale|gallery' even when 'featured' is present.
l2_priority: Run the live (Claude) EB-1A screening with a real biennale/gallery line and assert it maps to Artistic exhibitions, not Press.

title: Landing certificate and best-path are a keyword read, not the model — won't name her real evidence (disclosed)
expected: The verdict reflects what she actually pasted and names her real evidence.
got: The anonymous /api/qualify/preview and /api/qualify/preview/best-path run the deterministic keyword mock even under LLM_ENGINE=claude (best-path/route.ts:57-60). The output uses generic templated 'what we found' lines, not her named biennale/competitions/monograph. This is honestly labelled an 'instant keyword read' with a soft gate to the deeper model read (InstantVerdict.tsx:271-277), so it is disclosed, not deceptive; the real model only runs at the authenticated /api/qualify step.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the labeling; no change required. Optionally strengthen the soft-gate copy for arts/exhibitions users so they know the keyword card under-reads exhibitions specifically.
l2_priority: Confirm the authenticated /api/qualify (Claude) run names her supplied specifics (biennale, gallery, competitions, monograph) in evidence/rationale.

title: Citation discipline + citation audit make a fabricated exhibition/competition/publication impossible to ship un-flagged
expected: Zero fabricated competitions, exhibitions, or publications; each section crosswalks to a correct EB-1A criterion.
got: buildDraftPrompt forbids inventing awards/publications/specifics and forbids case-law (drafting.ts:181-192); when exhibits exist, CITATION_RULE forbids citing any exhibit number not on file (drafting.ts:160-166). auditCitations quarantines any (Exhibit N) that doesn't resolve to a real on-file document and computes utilization (drafting.ts:593-608). Sections are one-per-qualifying-criterion by name, so 'Artistic exhibitions' / 'Scholarly articles' get their own headings.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the citation discipline and audit; they directly defend against the 'fabricated exhibition' peeve.
l2_priority: Confirm the live draft does not invent an exhibition/competition and that any (Exhibit N) resolves to a real vault document.

title: Draft prompt has no EB-1A/arts-hybrid framing; Artistic-exhibitions prose quality and the 'top of the field' totality read are unproven
expected: Sections read like an EB-1A specialist: the biennale/gallery argued as Artistic exhibitions for an architect, the monograph as scholarly articles, with the 'very top of the field' totality the EB-1A demands.
got: buildDraftPrompt is classification-generic: it inserts ${req.classification} but gives the model no EB-1A-specific or arts-hybrid guidance — no note that 'Artistic exhibitions' for an architect means a biennale/gallery showing of work, and no 'very top of the field' totality instruction (drafting.ts:174-208). Section quality rides entirely on the model's defaults from the criterion name + persisted evidence; not visible to static analysis.
suggested_acceptance: Add light per-classification framing to the draft prompt (e.g. an EB-1A note on the totality 'top of the field' standard and what Artistic exhibitions means for a design/build profession), without injecting facts.
l2_priority: Generate a full EB-1A draft on her grounded case and judge whether the Artistic-exhibitions and Scholarly-articles sections read like a senior EB-1A drafter, not generic filler.

title: Keyless mock categorizer (pack-order first-match) can swallow a biennale document into Press before Artistic exhibitions
expected: A biennale/gallery document lands in the 'Artistic exhibitions' bucket.
got: mockCategorize uses packFor(classification).criteria.find(first regex match) (evidence.ts:185-191), same pack-order issue as il-qual-01: 'Featured at the Venice Biennale' can match Press before Artistic exhibitions. Mock path only; under LLM_ENGINE=claude the model classifies against the named ten. A real 'Artistic exhibitions' bucket exists (EvidenceVault.tsx:55) and the doc can be manually refiled (EvidenceVault.tsx:259-270).
suggested_acceptance: Same mock-ordering fix as il-qual-01; behavior is correct under the real model and recoverable via manual refile.
l2_priority: Add a biennale/gallery document to an EB-1A vault under Claude and assert it categorizes into Artistic exhibitions.

title: EB-1A case file number is minted with an 'O1-' prefix regardless of classification
expected: A green-card EB-1A case is labelled in a way that does not read as an O-1.
got: newFileNumber() always returns `O1-${random}` (petitions.ts:54-56), so an EB-1A case shows 'File No O1-48xx' on the masthead (CaseDetailView.tsx:108-109). The engine treats the case as EB-1A everywhere else; only the cosmetic file number says O1. For a petitioner who chose EB-1A precisely because it is the green card (not an O-1), the label is mildly jarring.
suggested_acceptance: Derive the file-number prefix from the classification (e.g. 'EB1-' for EB-1A) so the label matches the program.
l2_priority: Visually confirm the file-number prefix on a live EB-1A case; low priority.

title: No 'architect' profession in professions.ts; /visa/eb-1a/architect 404s (marketing surface, unreachable from her journeys)
expected: An architect could land on a profession-tuned EB-1A page (and the journeys never default to a wrong profession).
got: PROFESSIONS has only software-engineer, researcher, founder, designer, artist (professions.ts:26-113) — no 'architect'. The only 'architect' string in src/ is the substring 'architecture' in a sample (professions.ts:38). /visa/[classification]/[profession] calls notFound() for unknown slugs (page.tsx:73), so /visa/eb-1a/architect 404s. professions.ts is consumed ONLY by the SEO atelier and sitemap — it is not part of qualify/draft/evidence/track, which never key on profession. So this is a marketing content gap, unreachable from her core journeys, with no journey impact.
suggested_acceptance: Add an 'architect' profession entry (intro + arts/scholarly/exhibitions-tuned examples) to capture the EB-1A architect SEO landing; nearest current fits are 'designer'/'artist'. Low priority — does not affect the screening or draft.
l2_priority: None for the core journeys; optionally verify /visa/eb-1a/architect either exists or cleanly 404s.
